steps.title: Express the total number of animals
steps.content: Let $r$ be the number of rabbits and $h$ be the number of hamsters. The total number of animals cared for is $r+h$.

steps.title: Translate "at most 58" into an inequality
steps.content: The phrase "at most 58" means the total is less than or equal to 58, so $r+h \le 58$.

steps.title: Match against the choices
steps.content:
Compare to each option:
  • A) $r-h \le 58$ uses subtraction, not the sum of animals — eliminate.
  • B) $r-h \ge 58$ uses subtraction and the wrong direction — eliminate.
  • C) $r+h \ge 58$ has the correct sum but reverses the direction (this says at least 58) — eliminate.
  • D) $r+h \le 58$ correctly expresses "the total is at most 58."
That confirms D is correct.
